mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress forward earnings multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25pp.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns exceed zero in >70% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand growth across durable and non-durable sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate a positive rank correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects resolving supply chain bottlenecks and improving corporate input costs, which expands profit margins and drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with lagging policy rate hikes creates a negative real interest rate environment that forces aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in >65% of triggered months despite the inflation-rate divergence.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payrolls growth signals sustained labor demand and wage growth, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of months where payroll growth exceeds the 300k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing industrial production and rising consumer prices indicates cost-push inflation and supply constraints that erode corporate margins, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, with positive 20-day forward returns occurring in >65% of triggered instances.
